scope: read-only primary domain routing/deploy target diagnosis; no edits, no deploy, no Stripe calls, no admin runtime calls, no DB writes

summary.likelyCause: fimamacro.com and www.fimamacro.com are not routed to the Render backend service that now contains the v1.0.127 public tree. They still point to IONOS hosting with stale static files.

renderCustomDomainAttachment.fimamacro.comAttachedToBackend: no or DNS not configured to backend; live DNS/headers do not route to Render
renderCustomDomainAttachment.wwwAttachedToBackend: no or DNS not configured to backend; live DNS/headers do not route to Render
renderCustomDomainAttachment.apiAttachedToBackend: yes; api.fimamacro.com resolves to fima-backend-aqmi.onrender.com and serves the deployed backend/static files
renderCustomDomainAttachment.renderDashboardChecked: not directly accessible from shell without credentials; diagnosis is based on DNS, HTTP headers, and live asset behavior

ownershipDiagnosis.likelyPrimaryDomainHost: IONOS web hosting / Apache document root
ownershipDiagnosis.reasonCacheOnlyIssueUnlikely: DNS for fimamacro.com/www resolves to IONOS IPs and headers show Apache, while api.fimamacro.com resolves to Render and serves v127 immediately.

safestFixPlan: Do not run runtime jobs or Stripe/admin actions.
Decide desired hosting model for fimamacro.com.

If the backend Render service should serve the website, attach fimamacro.com and www.fimamacro.com as custom domains on the fima-backend Render service, then update IONOS DNS records away from 217.160.0.47 / 2001:8d8:100f:f000::200 to the Render-provided targets.
If IONOS should continue serving the primary website, upload/deploy the v1.0.127 public tree to the IONOS web root instead of relying on the backend repo.
If a separate website service exists outside this shell, identify its repo/service and apply the v1.0.127 public tree there.
After DNS/deploy change, verify fimamacro.com root, pricing, terms, privacy, success, dashboard, and /assets/fima-preview-v127.png?v=127 again.
Purge IONOS/CDN/browser cache only after the routing/deploy target is correct; cache purge alone will not fix DNS pointing to the wrong host.
nextRecommendedStep: Priority 2J: choose hosting target and either move fimamacro.com/www DNS to the Render backend service or deploy the v1.0.127 public tree to the current IONOS web root, then rerun live verification.
